The '''Government House''', also known as '''the Dark Palace'''<ref name="Strategy Guide">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>, was a building built on the summit of the [[Barons Knoll]] hill in the city of [[Barons Hed]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]].

==History==
[[File:8t88_4.jpg|thumb|left|[[8t88/Legends|8t88]] with his pet in Government House.]]
After the [[Subjugation of Sulon]] ([[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]) by [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]<ref name="Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref> the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] became a [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of the [[moon/Legends|moon]] for a short time, during which he built the Palace. After Jerec departed, it was left in the hands of [[Unidentified majordomo (Jerec)|his majordomo]] who continued to serve the executives who succeeded Jerec.<ref name="RA">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Jerec was after the [[Valley of the Jedi]] and some of his [[Seven Dark Jedi]] returned to Sulon in order to steal the [[Map to the Valley of the Jedi|map]] to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] from the [[Katarn family compound|Katarn homestead]]; the tiles of the map were then transported to the Palace. Meanwhile the [[information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[droid/Legends|droid]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] working for Jerec, [[Mission_to_Nar_Shaddaa_(5_ABY)|had failed]] to make [[Kyle Katarn]] cooperate, losing an [[limb/Legends|arm]] in the process; he retreated to Sulon, was hosted in the palace as a dignitary on a personal mission for Lord Jerec with his pet [[hornagaunt]] [[Grendel]]. During his stay there the 8t88 was served by [[human/Legends|humans]] [[Rol (Human)|Rol]] and [[Trox_(bodyguard)|Trox]], and the [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Hontho]]. The majordomo, irritated by the droid's bossy attitude and contempt for biologicals, assigned to him the coat room<ref name="RAAD" /> as a "guest suite"; an esoteric jest he hoped would impress the staff.<ref name="RA" />

Indeed, after a spare arm from another [[model 88-series administration droid]] was transported there, 8t88 arranged several tools on the room's table and installed the arm on himself. Then he decided to test his new arm, he summoned the "fool in charge" and inquired about his guest room's "pedigree". The majordomo initially attempted to persist that it was a VIP suite practical for the droid's job but the droid made clear he knew the room's role. After making an example of him, his screams scaring off the [[bird/Legends|birds]] from the nearby eaves.<ref name="RA" />
[[File:MaptoRuusanassembledby8t88.jpg|thumb|Kyle Katarn confronts 8t88 over the assembled pieces of the Map.]]
8t88 later moved into a more luxurious study with a [[wood/Legends|wooden]] floor, an ornately carved table and a thronelike chair, where he continued his work on the Map. Having transmitted his [[Holomap/Legends|3d reconstruction]] to Jerec onboard the ''[[Vengeance_(Vengeance-class)|Vengeance]]'', his minion [[Yun]] was sent back to Sulon with a detachment of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] tasked to destroy the original map.<ref name="RAAD" /> Meanwhile Katarn was looking for the Map and sneaked into the tower, killing Rol, Trox and Hontho. He found 8t88, but was too late as Yun emerged from an elevator on the ceiling, jumped on the table and destroyed the tiles of the Map with [[Yun's lightsaber|his lightsaber]]. He then attacked Katarn, giving 8t88 some time to leave. It was Katarn's first victory against a Dark Jedi, but spared the Dark Jedi's life. The youth escaped by floating towards the ceiling, and just then Kyle realised that 8t88 had also fled. A shuttle was leaving the Tower just when [[Jan Ors]] was circling the roof with the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''.<ref name="RA" />

Just then Stormtroopers stormed into the room, but seeing "a Jedi" deflecting their shots with [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber|his lightsaber]] they attempted to retreat; a [[Non-commissioned_officer/Legends|noncom]] kneecapped one of them to persuade them to fall back. Kyle made his way to a stairwell and to the roof platform where he was picked by the ''Crow'', and then followed 88's shuttle to the south towards the [[Fuel City]].<ref name="RA" />

==Layout and architecture==
Constructed from stone and reached a tall height, which combined with its location on top of the city's central hill gave it a commanding view of the city and inspired awe to the populace. Weapons emplacements protected the building.<ref name="RA" />

The residences [[High Town|around it]], built at or near the hill, belonged to the wealthiest citizens.<ref name="RA" />

The building contained luxurius guest rooms, also a ballroom, adjacent to which was a humble coat room, a dreary location without windows.<ref name="RA" /><ref name="RAAD">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref>
